content:
{
  "title": "Function Signature Synthesis Prompting: Enhancing LLM Code Generation via Explicit API Decomposition",
  "problem": "Current LLM code generation prompts often provide ambiguous or underspecified requirements, leading to hallucinated APIs or brittle outputs. There is a need for prompt methods that guide models to generate more accurate and maintainable code, especially when interfacing with complex or unfamiliar APIs.",
  "motivation": "LLMs frequently hallucinate function signatures or misuse APIs, particularly for less common libraries or novel problem statements. Prompting methods that more explicitly scaffold the API usage context have shown promise, but are underexplored. Improved prompting could significantly reduce integration errors and accelerate developer adoption of AI-assisted code generation.",
  "method": "We propose a two-stage prompting method: (1) First, prompt the LLM to synthesize a structured list of all function signatures, classes, and imported modules relevant for the task, inferred from the natural language description. (2) Then, feed both the original task and the synthesized API decomposition as a system-level preamble before eliciting full code generation. This structured priming is hypothesized to focus LLM attention on compositional API usage and reduce spurious hallucinations. The method is model- and language-agnostic, requiring only prompt engineering.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on HumanEval, MBPP, and APPS datasets, comparing against standard code generation prompts and chain-of-thought baselines. Metrics include functional correctness, API usage accuracy, and hallucination rate. We will also analyze signature extraction fidelity and ablation of the two-stage process."
}
